name: Painted Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream butter; gradually add sugar, beating until light and fluffy.
2. Add eggs one at a time, beating after each addition.
3. Add milk and vanilla, mix well.
4. Combine flour, baking powder, and baking soda, then add to the creamed mixture, stirring until blended.
5. Shape dough into 2 balls, wrap each in waxed paper, and chill for 4 hours.
6. Work with half of the dough at a time; store the remainder in the refrigerator.
7. Roll dough to 1/8 inch thickness on floured waxed paper, cut with 2-3 inch cookie cutters, and transfer to lightly-greased cookie sheets.
8. Paint assorted designs on cookies using small art brush and egg yolk paint.
9. Bake at 375°F for 6-8 minutes; cool on racks.
Egg Yolk Paint:
1. Beat one egg yolk with 1/4 teaspoon water.
2. Divide mixture evenly into two custard cups.
3. Tint as desired with food coloring.
4. Keep paint covered until ready to use. If it thickens, add a few drops of water and stir well.
Note: Prepare one recipe for egg yolk paint for every 2 colors desired. Makes about 1.5 tablespoons, enough for approximately 5 dozen cookies.
